# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

name: ci-cd-fast

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: write

env:
  NODE_ENV: production

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies (fast)
        run: npm ci --no-audit --no-fund

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build --if-present

      - name: Package artifact
        run: |
          mkdir -p dist_artifact
          if [ -d dist ]; then cp -R dist dist_artifact/; fi
          if [ -d build ]; then cp -R build dist_artifact/; fi
          if [ -f package.json ]; then cp package.json dist_artifact/; fi
          if [ -f package-lock.json ]; then cp package-lock.json dist_artifact/; fi
          tar -czf app.tar.gz -C dist_artifact .
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: app
          path: app.tar.gz
          retention-days: 1

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: app

      - name: Unpack
        run: |
          mkdir -p release
          tar -xzf app.tar.gz -C release

      - name: Deploy via SSH (rsync)
        env:
          SSH_HOST: ${{ secrets.SSH_HOST }}
          SSH_USER: ${{ secrets.SSH_USER }}
          SSH_PORT: ${{ secrets.SSH_PORT }}
          SSH_KEY: ${{ secrets.SSH_KEY }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
        run: |
          test -n "$SSH_HOST" && test -n "$SSH_USER" && test -n "$SSH_KEY" && test -n "$DEPLOY_PATH"
          PORT="${SSH_PORT:-22}"
          install -m 700 -d ~/.ssh
          printf "%s" "$SSH_KEY" > ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          ssh-keyscan -p "$PORT" -H "$SSH_HOST" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts
          rsync -az --delete -e "ssh -p $PORT" release/ "$SSH_USER@$SSH_HOST:$DEPLOY_PATH/"
          ssh -p "$PORT" "$SSH_USER@$SSH_HOST" "cd '$DEPLOY_PATH' && if [ -f package.json ]; then npm ci --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund; fi && if [ -f ecosystem.config.js ]; then pm2 reload ecosystem.config.js --update-env || pm2 start ecosystem.config.js; elif command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then sudo systemctl restart app || true; fi"
